title: Contributing to entsoeapi
text: Fork repository create branch main. Install development dependencies: devtools::install_dev_deps(). Make changes. Keep PR focused single concern. Add update tests — package targets 100% test coverage. Run full check suite locally pushing: Open pull request main describe changed .

title: Contributing to entsoeapi
text: Follow existing code style (snake_case, cli:: user-facing messages, checkmate:: input validation). New user-facing functions must @examplesIf blocks (see existing functions pattern). exported functions need @return tag roxygen documentation. ENTSO-E security tokens must never appear code, tests, fixtures — use ENTSOE_PAT environment variable.

title: Architecture: API Pipeline, XML Engine & Caching
text: document describes three interconnected internal systems power every data-retrieval function entsoeapi: API Request Pipeline — validates user input, builds ENTSO-E query URL, sends HTTP request, handles errors, transparently paginates oversized queries. XML--Table Engine — converts raw XML (lists XML documents paginated responses) clean, typed, enriched R tibbles. Caching System — stores EIC code tables lookup tables memory one hour avoid redundant downloads session. three systems form strict linear pipeline. user-facing function (e.g., load_actual_total()) calls API pipeline, produces one XML documents. documents handed XML--table engine, calls caching system enrich results human-readable names definitions returning tibble user.

title: Architecture: API Pipeline, XML Engine & Caching
code: User function   |   v [Validate params / url_posixct_format]   |   v [Build query string]   |   v [api_req_safe]  <-----------------------------+   |                                           |   v                                           | [api_req --> GET request, 60s timeout]        |   |                                           |   |                                           |   +-- HTTP 200 / zip --> [read_zipped_xml] ---+--> [extract_response]   |                                           |          |   +-- HTTP 200 / xml --> [resp_body_xml] -----+          v   |                                                [xml_to_table,   |                                                 per document]   +-- error 503 -------> [req_retry, 3x / 10s]           |   |                            |                         v   |                            v                   [extract_leaf/twig/branch]   |                      [cli_abort if all fail]         |   |                                                      v   +-- error HTML ------> [cli_abort]               [type conversions]   |                                                      |   +-- exceeds max -----> [calc_offset_urls,              v                           pagination]              [my_snakecase                                |                    column rename]                                v                         |                          [api_req] (loop)]               v                                                    [tidy_or_not:                                                     A01 / A03 curve] v                                          [return cached       [download CSV/XML,                                           lookup table]        cache result] title: Architecture: API Pipeline, XML Engine & Caching
text: Location: R/en_load.R, R/en_generation.R, R/en_transmission.R, R/en_market.R, R/en_outages.R, R/en_balancing.R Every public function follows four-step structure: Step 1 — Argument validation. parameter checked checkmate network call made. Common checks: EIC codes: two-stage check — checkmate::assert_string() first enforces exactly 16 characters matching ^[-Z0-9-]*$, assert_eic() verifies 16th character correct weighted-modulo-37 checksum first 15 characters (see ). Security token: non-empty string (sourced Sys.getenv("ENTSOE_PAT")) Time ranges: difference period_end period_start must exceed 365 days Categorical parameters (e.g., business_type, process_type): validated allowed values via checkmate::assert_choice() there_is_provider() (R/utils.R, exported): lightweight connectivity check sends dummy request ENTSO-E API returns TRUE server responds HTTP 401 (meaning endpoint reachable token rejected expected). Returns FALSE internet connection available server unreachable. primary role @examplesIf guard package documentation, ensuring examples executed API accessible. EIC checksum validation — assert_eic() possible_eic_chars (R/utils.R): ENTSO-E EIC standard defines check character position 16. named integer vector possible_eic_chars maps 37 allowed characters (0–9, –Z, -) integers 0–36. assert_eic() computes weighted sum first 15 characters (weights 16 2), derives expected check character via (36 - (sum - 1) %% 37) + 1, aborts informative message match actual 16th character. optional null_ok = TRUE argument allows NULL pass validation (used functions accept optional EIC parameter). function exported; called internally immediately checkmate::assert_string() EIC check. Step 2 — Timestamp conversion. url_posixct_format() (R/utils.R) converts user-supplied period_start / period_end format required API: YYYYMMDDHHMM UTC. Accepts POSIXct objects character strings nine common formats. Aborts clear message input parsed. Warns character input interpreted UTC. Step 3 — Query string assembly. function hard-codes ENTSO-E document type process type codes endpoint, appends user-supplied EIC code(s) converted timestamps. Optional parameters (e.g., business_type) appended conditionally (!.null(...)). Step 4 — Pipeline invocation.
code: en_cont_list <- api_req_safe(query_string, security_token) extract_response(content = en_cont_list, tidy_output = tidy_output)

title: Architecture: API Pipeline, XML Engine & Caching
text: Location: R/utils.R one-liner wraps api_req() package-local safely() helper (lightweight tryCatch wrapper). R-level exceptions caught returned list(result = NULL, error = <condition>) rather halting execution. standardised return shape extract_response() expects.
code: api_req_safe <- safely(api_req)

title: Architecture: API Pipeline, XML Engine & Caching
text: Location: R/utils.R core HTTP function. Steps: URL construction. Assembles full URL package-level constants defined R/constants.R: Scheme: .api_scheme ("https://") Domain: .api_domain ("web-api.tp.entsoe.eu/") Path: .api_name ("api?") Appends query_string &securityToken={token} Logs URL console token replaced <...> prevent credential leakage. Request configuration. Uses httr2::request() : Method: GET Verbose: response headers (req_verbose(header_req=FALSE, header_resp=TRUE)) Timeout: .req_timeout seconds (60, defined R/constants.R) Retry: 3 attempts 10-second backoff, triggered HTTP 503 (Service Unavailable) responses. HTTP errors retried. guards transient server-side overload ENTSO-E platform. Execution. Sent via safely(httr2::req_perform) (package-local wrapper) network errors captured, thrown. HTTP 200 — response routing. application/zip application/octet-stream: body saved temp file, decompressed read_zipped_xml(). text/xml application/xml: parsed directly httr2::resp_body_xml(encoding = "UTF-8"). Unknown content-type: aborts informative message. HTTP errors — error handling. See section 1.4. Returns either single xml_document, list xml_document objects (paginated zipped responses), calls cli::cli_abort().

title: Architecture: API Pipeline, XML Engine & Caching
text: Location: calc_offset_urls() R/utils.R ENTSO-E API returns XML error reason code 999 message indicating result set exceeds allowed maximum, api_req() automatically splits request smaller chunks: error message parsed regular expression extract requested allowed document counts. number offset requests needed calculated: ceiling(docs_requested / docs_allowed). offset query built stripping existing &offset= original query string appending &offset=0, &offset=N, &offset=2N, … api_req() calls recursively offset query string. responses collected returned list, XML--table engine processes element element. Pagination suppressed (request aborted instead) endpoints known support offsets, identified six hard-coded regular expression patterns covering document types A63, A65, B09, A91, A92, A94 specific business storage types.

title: Architecture: API Pipeline, XML Engine & Caching
text: Location: R/utils.R Called API returns zip archive. Decompresses temp file safely(utils::unzip) (using package-local wrapper), reads extracted XML file xml2::read_xml(). Returns list xml_document objects — shape paginated response, extract_response() handles identically.

title: Architecture: API Pipeline, XML Engine & Caching
text: Location: R/utils.R Entry point called every user-facing function. Accepts list(result, error) api_req_safe(). $error NULL: re-throws error cli::cli_abort(). $result list (paginated zipped): iterates lapply(), calling xml_to_table() element, showing progress bar, combines results dplyr::bind_rows() converts tibble. $result single xml_document: calls xml_to_table() directly. Returns tibble, NULL API returned data.

title: Architecture: API Pipeline, XML Engine & Caching
text: Location: R/utils.R Core orchestrator. Receives single xml_document returns tibble running fixed transformation sequence: XML parsing → raw wide data frame Date/time column merging Type conversions (DateTime, numeric) Column name normalization Time series restructuring Metadata enrichment (type names, EIC names, definitions) Column whitelist filtering Row ordering

title: Architecture: API Pipeline, XML Engine & Caching
text: Location: extract_leaf_twig_branch(), extract_nodesets() R/utils.R ENTSO-E XML schema uses three nesting levels, engine labels: extract_nodesets() converts XML nodesets data.table objects using xml2::as_list(), constructing dotted column names element hierarchy (e.g., TimeSeries.mRID). NULL values become NA_character_.

title: Architecture: API Pipeline, XML Engine & Caching
text: Location: R/utils.R Two-pass renaming: Pass 1 — domain-specific substitutions (applied snakecase conversion): Pass 2 — standard snakecase via snakecase::to_snake_case(), followed cleanup passes collapse redundant fragments (e.g., psr_type_psr_type → psr_type).

title: Architecture: API Pipeline, XML Engine & Caching
text: Location: R/utils.R ENTSO-E API encodes time series start timestamp plus resolution sequence positional data points — per-point timestamps XML. tidy_or_not() reconstructs absolute timestamps offers two output shapes: Curve type A01 (regular intervals): Points evenly spaced; timestamps calculated time_interval_start + (position - 1) × resolution. Curve type A03 (irregular / broken intervals): positions may missing. engine builds complete positional frame performs full join fill gaps, carrying forward last observed value. Supported resolutions: PT4S, PT1M, PT15M, PT30M, PT60M, P1D, P7D, P1M, P1Y. tidy_output = TRUE (default): One row per data point. ts_point_dt_start column contains reconstructed timestamp. Internal bookkeeping columns (ts_point_position, ts_resolution_*, ) removed. tidy_output = FALSE: One row per time period. data points nested ts_point list-column. reconstructed ts_point_dt_start column removed.

title: Architecture: API Pipeline, XML Engine & Caching
text: Three functions run sequence time series restructuring, joining additional columns: add_type_names() (R/utils.R) — joins human-readable definitions built-package data tables (e.g., business_types, asset_types, process_types) using lookup_merge(). Produces _def suffix columns alongside code column (e.g., ts_business_type → ts_business_type_def). add_eic_names() (R/utils.R) — joins EIC code long names area_eic() resource_object_eic() (cached; see section 3) using lookup_merge(). Produces _name suffix columns alongside _mrid column (e.g., ts_in_domain_mrid → ts_in_domain_name). add_definitions() (R/utils.R) — joins definitions: auction categories, flow directions, reason codes (multi-code merging via " - " separator), object aggregation types.

title: Architecture: API Pipeline, XML Engine & Caching
text: enrichment, xml_to_table() applies hard-coded whitelist ~140 allowed column names. column list silently dropped. prevents internal XML artefacts leaking user-visible output keeps API stable across ENTSO-E schema changes. Rows sorted : created_date_time, ts_mrid, ts_business_type, ts_mkt_psr_type, ts_time_interval_start, ts_point_dt_start (present).

title: Architecture: API Pipeline, XML Engine & Caching
text: package maintains two independent -memory caches, 1-hour maximum age: cachem::cache_mem(max_age = .max_age) objects, .max_age package-level constant 3600 (defined R/constants.R). max age user-configurable.

title: Architecture: API Pipeline, XML Engine & Caching
text: Via mh (one key per EIC function): Via m (used inside XML--table engine): cached: API responses. Every call load_actual_total(), gen_per_prod_type(), etc. makes fresh HTTP request. slow, stable reference data (EIC registries, type definitions) cached.

title: Architecture: API Pipeline, XML Engine & Caching
text: EIC functions use template: cli::cli_alert_info() calls make cache source visible user console.
code: cache_key <- "unique_key_name"  if (mh$exists(key = cache_key)) {   res_df <- mh$get(key = cache_key, missing = fallback_expr)   cli_alert_info("pulling {f} file from cache") } else {   cli_alert_info("downloading {f} file ...")   res_df <- download_and_transform()   mh$set(key = cache_key, value = res_df) }

title: Architecture: API Pipeline, XML Engine & Caching
text: add_eic_names() calls get_resource_object_eic() (cache m), fetches area EIC names inline (cache m, falling back area_eic() cache miss, uses cache mh). means underlying data may stored two levels simultaneously: mh holds full EIC tibble (columns). m holds narrowed subset (EicCode + EicLongName ) ready joining. caches warm, subsequent API calls within session perform zero downloads EIC enrichment.

title: Architecture: API Pipeline, XML Engine & Caching
text: Invalidation entirely automatic. cachem expires entries silently max_age seconds. manual cache-clear API, environment variable disable caching, cache versioning. Restarting R session clears caches.

title: Architecture: API Pipeline, XML Engine & Caching
text: following traces call load_actual_total():
code: load_actual_total(eic, period_start, period_end, tidy_output = TRUE)   │   ├─ checkmate: assert EIC format, token presence, ≤365-day range   ├─ url_posixct_format(period_start / period_end) → "YYYYMMDDHHMM" UTC   ├─ Build query string: "documentType=A65&processType=A16&outBiddingZone_Domain=…"   │   └─ api_req_safe(query_string, security_token)        └─ api_req()             ├─ Build URL: https://web-api.tp.entsoe.eu/api?{query}&securityToken=<...>             ├─ GET, 60s timeout, retry 3× on 503 (10s backoff), log masked URL             ├─ HTTP 200 / text/xml → resp_body_xml()  [or zip → read_zipped_xml()]             └─ HTTP error → calc_offset_urls() + recurse  [or cli_abort()]      │      └─ extract_response(list(result, error), tidy_output)           ├─ error? → cli_abort()           ├─ list of XML? → imap + progress bar           │           └─ xml_to_table(xml_doc, tidy_output)                ├─ extract_leaf_twig_branch() → raw wide data frame                ├─ Merge date+time columns                ├─ Convert DateTime → POSIXct(UTC), numeric columns → numeric                ├─ my_snakecase() → normalised column names                ├─ tidy_or_not() → one row per data point (A01/A03 handled)            ├─ add_type_names()  → join built-in type tables (no network)            ├─ add_eic_names()   → get_resource_object_eic() [cache m]                ├─ add_definitions() → join built-in definition tables (no network)                ├─ Filter to whitelist columns                └─ Sort rows           │           └─ dplyr::bind_rows() + as_tbl() if multiple XML docs      │      └─ tibble returned to user  (or NULL)

what: Calculate the daily minimum and maximum prices and the spread
title: Day-Ahead Price Spread Vignette
code: da_spreads <- da_prices |>   mutate(     ts_point_dt_start = with_tz(time = ts_point_dt_start, tzone = "CET")   ) |>   mutate(     ts_point_date = as.Date(x = ts_point_dt_start, tz = "CET")   ) |>   summarise(     min_price = min(ts_point_price_amount, na.rm = TRUE),     max_price = max(ts_point_price_amount, na.rm = TRUE),     .by = ts_point_date   ) |>   mutate(price_spread = max_price - min_price)  da_spreads |>   kbl(format = "pipe") |>   cat(sep = "\n") #> |ts_point_date | min_price| max_price| price_spread| #> |:-------------|---------:|---------:|------------:| #> |2026-01-01    |     -4.73|    109.53|       114.26| #> |2026-01-02    |     -8.31|    110.54|       118.85| #> |2026-01-03    |     51.15|    114.45|        63.30| #> |2026-01-04    |     81.85|    123.50|        41.65| #> |2026-01-05    |     82.58|    246.55|       163.97| #> |2026-01-06    |    107.75|    189.91|        82.16| #> |2026-01-07    |     80.55|    326.58|       246.03|

what: Plot the daily minimum and maximum prices and the spread
title: Day-Ahead Price Spread Vignette
code: print(   ggplot(data = da_spreads) +     geom_segment(       mapping = aes(         x = ts_point_date,         xend = ts_point_date,         y = min_price,         yend = max_price       ),       linewidth = 10,       color = "tomato"     ) +     geom_text(       mapping = aes(         x = ts_point_date,         y = max_price + 10,         label = price_spread       ),       size = 4     ) +     scale_x_date(       breaks = da_spreads$ts_point_date,       date_labels = "%Y-%m-%d"     ) +     theme_minimal() +     labs(       title = "Polish Daily Spread of Day-Ahead Prices",       subtitle = paste("PT15M,", from_ts, till_ts),       x = "delivery date",       y = "day-ahead price spread"     ) +     theme(       axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)     ) )

title: Generation Mix Analysis

title: Generation Mix Analysis
text: Use gen_per_prod_type() get generation data:
code: cli_h1("Fetching Generation Data") #>  #> ── Fetching Generation Data ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  # Define time range from_ts <- ymd(x = "2024-06-01", tz = "CET") till_ts <- from_ts + days(7)  cli_inform("Period: {from_ts} to {till_ts}") #> Period: 2024-06-01 to 2024-06-08  # Fetch generation by production type de_generation <- gen_per_prod_type(   eic = de_zone,   period_start = from_ts,   period_end = till_ts,   tidy_output = TRUE ) #>  #> ── API call ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> → https://web-api.tp.entsoe.eu/api?documentType=A75&processType=A16&in_Domain=10Y1001A1001A83F&periodStart=202405312200&periodEnd=202406072200&securityToken=<...> #> <- HTTP/2 200  #> <- date: Wed, 08 Apr 2026 13:09:55 GMT #> <- content-type: text/xml #> <- content-disposition: inline; filename="Aggregated Generation per Type_202405312200-202406072200.xml" #> <- x-content-type-options: nosniff #> <- x-xss-protection: 0 #> <- vary: accept-encoding #> <- content-encoding: gzip #> <- strict-transport-security: max-age=15724800; includeSubDomains #> <- #> ✔ response has arrived #> ✔ Additional type names have been added! #>  #> ── public download ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ pulling Y_eicCodes.csv file from cache #> ✔ Additional eic names have been added! #> ✔ Additional definitions have been added!  cli_alert_success("Retrieved {nrow(de_generation)} data points") #> ✔ Retrieved 12768 data points

title: Generation Mix Analysis
text: Check production types present:
code: cli_h1("Production Types in Dataset") #>  #> ── Production Types in Dataset ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  de_generation |>   summarize(     n_points = n(),     total_mwh = sum(x = ts_point_quantity, na.rm = TRUE) |> round(),     .by = c(ts_mkt_psr_type, ts_mkt_psr_type_def)   ) |>   arrange(desc(total_mwh)) |>   kbl(format = "pipe") |>   cat(sep = "\n") #> |ts_mkt_psr_type |ts_mkt_psr_type_def                                  | n_points| total_mwh| #> what: Stacked Area Chart of Generation Mix
title: Generation Mix Analysis
text: Visualize composition generation mix time:
code: # Prepare data for visualization gen_plot_data <- de_generation |>   mutate(     ts_point_dt_start = with_tz(ts_point_dt_start, tzone = "CET"),     date = as_date(x = ts_point_dt_start, tz = "CET"),     hour = with_tz(time = ts_point_dt_start, tzone = "CET") |> hour()   ) |>   summarize(     total_mwh = sum(ts_point_quantity, na.rm = TRUE),     .by = c(date, hour, ts_mkt_psr_type_def)   ) |>   mutate(datetime = ymd_h(paste(date, hour), tz = "CET"))  # Define colors for production types production_colors <- c(   "Biomass" = "#FF6347",   "Fossil Brown coal/Lignite" = "#8B4513",   "Fossil Coal-derived gas" = "#D93333",   "Fossil Gas" = "#4682B4",   "Fossil Hard coal" = "#2F4F4F",   "Fossil Oil" = "#256232",   "Geothermal" = "#00FFFF",   "Hydro Run-of-river head installation" = "#4169E1",   "Hydro-electric pure pumped storage head installation" = "#1E90FF",   "Hydro-electric storage head installation" = "#87CEEB",   "Other renewable" = "#90EE90",   "Other unspecified" = "#808080",   "Solar unspecified" = "#FFD700",   "Waste" = "#A9A9A9",   "Wind Offshore" = "#00CED1",   "Wind Onshore" = "#32CD32" )  # Create stacked area plot ggplot(   data = gen_plot_data,   mapping = aes(     x = datetime,     y = total_mwh,     fill = ts_mkt_psr_type_def   ) ) +   geom_area(position = "stack") +   scale_fill_manual(     values = production_colors,     name = NULL   ) +   labs(     title = "Germany: Daily Generation Mix Over Time (Stacked Area)",     subtitle = "June 2024 - first 7 days",     x = "Date",     y = "Generation (MWh)"   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom") +   scale_x_datetime(date_breaks = "1 day", date_labels = "%b %d") +   scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma)

title: Generation Mix Analysis
text: Compare average daily generation production type:
code: # Calculate daily averages daily_avg <- de_generation |>   mutate(date = as.Date(x = ts_point_dt_start, tz = "CET")) |>   summarize(     avg_mw = mean(ts_point_quantity, na.rm = TRUE),     .by = c(date, ts_mkt_psr_type_def)   ) |>   summarize(     avg_daily_mwh = mean(avg_mw) * 24,     .by = ts_mkt_psr_type_def   ) |>   arrange(desc(avg_daily_mwh))  # Bar chart ggplot(   data = daily_avg,   mapping = aes(     x = reorder(ts_mkt_psr_type_def, avg_daily_mwh),     y = avg_daily_mwh   ) ) +   geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +   labs(     title = "Germany: Average Daily Generation by Production Type",     subtitle = "June 2024",     x = "Production Type",     y = "Average Daily Generation (MWh)"   ) +   scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +   theme_minimal() +   coord_flip()

title: Generation Mix Analysis
text: Calculate share renewable generation:
code: # Define renewable types renewable_types <- c(   "Biomass",   "Solar unspecified",   "Wind Offshore",   "Wind Onshore",   "Hydro Run-of-river head installation",   "Hydro-electric pure pumped storage head installation",   "Hydro-electric storage head installation",   "Geothermal",   "Other renewable" )  # Calculate penetration gen_summary <- de_generation |>   mutate(     is_renewable = ts_mkt_psr_type_def %in% renewable_types,     date = as.Date(x = ts_point_dt_start, tz = "CET")   ) |>   summarize(     total_gen = sum(ts_point_quantity, na.rm = TRUE),     renewable_gen = sum(ts_point_quantity[is_renewable], na.rm = TRUE),     renewable_pct = renewable_gen / total_gen * 100,     .by = date   )  print(gen_summary) #> # A tibble: 7 × 4 #>   date       total_gen renewable_gen renewable_pct #>   <date>         <dbl>         <dbl>         <dbl> #> 1 2024-06-01  3867508.      2910125.          75.2 #> 2 2024-06-02  4224619.      3330698.          78.8 #> 3 2024-06-03  4417857.      2417481.          54.7 #> 4 2024-06-04  4457779.      2580900.          57.9 #> 5 2024-06-05  4973111.      3481453.          70.0 #> 6 2024-06-06  4791237.      3000533.          62.6 #> 7 2024-06-07  4525444.      2868269.          63.4  # Daily renewable share ggplot(   data = gen_summary,   mapping = aes(x = date, y = renewable_pct) ) +   geom_col(fill = "forestgreen", alpha = 0.7) +   labs(     title = "Germany: Daily Renewable Penetration",     subtitle = "June 2024 - first 7 days",     x = "Date",     y = "Renewable Share (%)"   ) +   ylim(0, 100) +   scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 day", date_labels = "%b %d") +   theme_minimal()

title: Generation Mix Analysis
text: Calculate capacity factors renewable generation:
code: # Get installed capacity (simplified - uses example values) cli_inform(   "Note: Full capacity analysis requires matching installed capacity data" ) #> Note: Full capacity analysis requires matching installed capacity data  # Estimate capacity factors for solar and wind capacity_estimates <- de_generation |>   filter(     ts_mkt_psr_type_def %in% c(       "Solar unspecified", "Wind Onshore", "Wind Offshore"     )   ) |>   mutate(date = as.Date(x = ts_point_dt_start, tz = "CET")) |>   summarize(     max_mw = max(ts_point_quantity, na.rm = TRUE),     avg_mw = mean(ts_point_quantity, na.rm = TRUE),     .by = c(date, ts_mkt_psr_type_def)   ) |>   mutate(capacity_factor = avg_mw / max_mw)  ggplot(   data = capacity_estimates,   mapping = aes(     x = date,     y = capacity_factor,     color = ts_mkt_psr_type_def   ) ) +   geom_line() +   facet_wrap(~ts_mkt_psr_type_def, scales = "free_y") +   labs(     title = "Germany: Estimated Capacity Factors by Day",     subtitle = "Ratio of average to peak generation",     x = "Date",     y = "Capacity Factor"   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(     legend.position = "bottom",     legend.title = element_blank()   ) +   ylim(0, 1)

title: Generation Mix Analysis
text: Compare generation mix different time periods:
code: # Fetch data for two weeks week1 <- gen_per_prod_type(   eic = de_zone,   period_start = lubridate::ymd(x = "2024-06-01", tz = "CET"),   period_end = lubridate::ymd(x = "2024-06-08", tz = "CET"),   tidy_output = TRUE ) |>   mutate(period = "Week 1") #>  #> ── API call ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> → https://web-api.tp.entsoe.eu/api?documentType=A75&processType=A16&in_Domain=10Y1001A1001A83F&periodStart=202405312200&periodEnd=202406072200&securityToken=<...> #> <- HTTP/2 200  #> <- date: Wed, 08 Apr 2026 13:10:01 GMT #> <- content-type: text/xml #> <- content-disposition: inline; filename="Aggregated Generation per Type_202405312200-202406072200.xml" #> <- x-content-type-options: nosniff #> <- x-xss-protection: 0 #> <- vary: accept-encoding #> <- content-encoding: gzip #> <- strict-transport-security: max-age=15724800; includeSubDomains #> <- #> ✔ response has arrived #> ✔ Additional type names have been added! #> ✔ Additional eic names have been added! #> ✔ Additional definitions have been added!  week2 <- gen_per_prod_type(   eic = de_zone,   period_start = lubridate::ymd(x = "2024-06-15", tz = "CET"),   period_end = lubridate::ymd(x = "2024-06-22", tz = "CET"),   tidy_output = TRUE ) |>   mutate(period = "Week 3") #>  #> ── API call ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> → https://web-api.tp.entsoe.eu/api?documentType=A75&processType=A16&in_Domain=10Y1001A1001A83F&periodStart=202406142200&periodEnd=202406212200&securityToken=<...> #> <- HTTP/2 200  #> <- date: Wed, 08 Apr 2026 13:10:05 GMT #> <- content-type: text/xml #> <- content-disposition: inline; filename="Aggregated Generation per Type_202406142200-202406212200.xml" #> <- x-content-type-options: nosniff #> <- x-xss-protection: 0 #> <- vary: accept-encoding #> <- content-encoding: gzip #> <- strict-transport-security: max-age=15724800; includeSubDomains #> <- #> ✔ response has arrived #> ✔ Additional type names have been added! #> ✔ Additional eic names have been added! #> ✔ Additional definitions have been added!  # Combine and compare combined <- bind_rows(week1, week2) |>   summarize(     total_gen = sum(ts_point_quantity, na.rm = TRUE),     .by = c(period, ts_mkt_psr_type_def)   ) |>   mutate(     pct = total_gen / sum(total_gen) * 100,     .by = period   )  # Plot comparison ggplot(   data = combined,   mapping = aes(x = ts_mkt_psr_type_def, y = pct, fill = period) ) +   geom_col(position = "dodge") +   coord_flip() +   labs(     title = "Germany: Generation Mix Comparison",     subtitle = "Week 1st vs Week 3rd of June 2024",     x = "Production Type",     y = "Share of Generation (%)"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Generation Mix Analysis
text: vignette demonstrated: Fetching generation data gen_per_prod_type() Understanding production type codes B01 B24 Visualizing generation mix stacked area charts Calculating renewable penetration time Capacity factor estimation renewable sources Comparing time periods identify trends entsoeapi package makes straightforward analyse European electricity generation data research, policy analysis, business intelligence.

title: Getting Started with entsoeapi
text: entsoeapi package provides standardized R interface ENTSO-E (European Network Transmission System Operators Electricity) Transparency Platform API. platform publishes electricity market, generation, load, transmission, outage, balancing data European countries. vignette guide : Setting package obtaining API access Checking connectivity platform news Finding Energy Identification Codes (EICs) areas interest Making first API call Understanding output structure

title: Getting Started with entsoeapi
text: use ENTSO-E Transparency Platform API, need security token: Register https://transparency.entsoe.eu/ registration, token available profile Set ENTSOE_PAT environment variable .Renviron file: persistent access, add line .Renviron file:
code: usethis::edit_r_environ() # In your ~/.Renviron file: ENTSOE_PAT=your_token_here

title: Getting Started with entsoeapi
text: Install development version GitHub: Load packages:
code: # Install from GitHub remotes::install_github("krose/entsoeapi")  # Or install from CRAN (when available) install.packages("entsoeapi") library(entsoeapi) suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(dplyr)) library(cli) suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(lubridate)) suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(kableExtra))

title: Getting Started with entsoeapi
text: there_is_provider() function checks ENTSO-E API reachable:
code: # Check if the API is accessible there_is_provider() #> [1] TRUE

title: Getting Started with entsoeapi
text: get_news() function fetches latest announcements ENTSO-E Transparency Platform RSS feed. useful checking planned maintenance windows data publication delays running batch queries: result returned invisibly tibble, can also capture filter :
code: # Show the latest 3 news items get_news(n = 3L) #>  #> ── ENTSO-E Transparency Platform News ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Transparency Platform Quarterly Newsletter subscription  ── #>  #> ℹ Tue, 07 Apr 2026 12:45:33 GMT #> Dear Transparency Platform users,We are pleased to introduce the Transparency Platform Quarterly Newsletter. This #> newsletter will cover topics such as feature releases, user group meeting announcements, planned events, and any #> service interruptions.To subscribe, please use the following LINK.Kind regards,Transparency Platform team #>  #> ── PSE: Republication of data under Article 12.1.f  ── #>  #> ℹ Fri, 03 Apr 2026 11:59:36 GMT #> Dear Transparency Platform users,Polskie Sieci Elektroenergetyczne (PSE) hereby informs stakeholders of the #> republication of data released pursuant to Article 12.1.f. The updated dataset now covers the period commencing on 19 #> March 2025. The data have been republished for both directions, and no netting has been applied.We extend our sincere #> apologies for any inconvenience this republication may have caused.Kind regards,Transparency Platform team on behalf of #> PSE #>  #> ── R3.19.0.3 on Thursday 02.04.2026 at 15:00 - 16:30 CEST ── #>  #> ℹ Wed, 01 Apr 2026 14:56:52 GMT #> Dear Transparency Platform Users,The deployment of TP Release R3.19.0.3 on the PROD environment is scheduled to begin #> on Thursday, 2nd April 2026 at 15:00 CEST.Please note that the platform will be unavailable for up to 90 minutes during #> this deployment window.Scope of Release R3.19.0.3:Publications & Filtering:Wrong resolution published in 11.1. Flow #> based processed (PT15M→PT60M)Time Horizon & Source published as codes for 17.1.B&C Volumes and Prices of the Contracted #> Balancing ReservesForecasted Transfer Capacities [11.1] data published with incorrect time zone10.1.A&B Unavailability #> Transmission Grid - Improvements to filters and sortingGUI Exports improvements:10.1.A&B Unavailability in Transmission #> Grid - Unexpected error while downloading data12.3.E - Aggregated Balancing Energy Bids – XML export results in #> errorGUI Performance Improvements:15.1.ABCD - Unavailability of Production & Generation Units chart doesn't #> load"Published Network Elements" data view errorsOther Marker Information[OMI] - unexpected error12.1.D Energy Prices #> for DE‑LU Data view errorREST API improvements:Use of the Transfer Capacity [12.1.A] returns wrong periodsAll Outages 4 #> parameters (periodStart, periodEnd, PeriodStartUpdate, PeriodEndUpdate) are ignored when MRID is included11.1 #> Continuous Evolution - Contract_MarketAgreement.Type should remain an optional parameter13.1.A - Redispatches #> [Internal, Cross Border] , 13.1.B - Contertrading - Corrections to attributes in XML downloads and API responseBest #> regards,Transparency Platform Team news <- get_news(n = 10L) news |>   subset(subset = grepl(pattern = "maintenance", x = title, ignore.case = TRUE))

title: Getting Started with entsoeapi
text: Energy Identification Codes (EICs) 16-character codes uniquely identify market participants, bidding zones, transmission lines, entities ENTSO-E platform. all_approved_eic() function returns comprehensive list approved EIC codes: area_eic() function provides focused lookup bidding zones:

title: Getting Started with entsoeapi
text: Let’s fetch day-ahead energy prices Germany. energy_prices() function queries market data endpoint:
code: # Define time range (one week of data) from_ts <- ymd("2026-01-01", tz = "CET") till_ts <- from_ts + weeks(1L)  cli_h1("Querying Day-Ahead Energy Prices") #>  #> ── Querying Day-Ahead Energy Prices ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── cli_inform("Period: {from_ts} to {till_ts}") #> Period: 2026-01-01 to 2026-01-08 cli_inform("Area: {germany_eic}") #> Area: 10Y1001A1001A82H  # Fetch the data da_prices <- energy_prices(   eic = germany_eic,   period_start = from_ts,   period_end = till_ts,   contract_type = "A01",  # Day-ahead   tidy_output = TRUE,   security_token = Sys.getenv("ENTSOE_PAT") ) #>  #> ── API call ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> → https://web-api.tp.entsoe.eu/api?documentType=A44&in_Domain=10Y1001A1001A82H&out_Domain=10Y1001A1001A82H&periodStart=202512312300&periodEnd=202601072300&contract_MarketAgreement.type=A01&securityToken=<...> #> <- HTTP/2 200  #> <- date: Wed, 08 Apr 2026 13:10:24 GMT #> <- content-type: text/xml #> <- content-disposition: inline; filename="Energy_Prices_202512312300-202601072300.xml" #> <- x-content-type-options: nosniff #> <- x-xss-protection: 0 #> <- vary: accept-encoding #> <- content-encoding: gzip #> <- strict-transport-security: max-age=15724800; includeSubDomains #> <- #> ✔ response has arrived #> ✔ Additional type names have been added! #>  #> ── public download ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ pulling Y_eicCodes.csv file from cache #> ✔ Additional eic names have been added!  cli_alert_success("Retrieved {nrow(da_prices)} rows") #> ✔ Retrieved 1344 rows

title: Getting Started with entsoeapi
text: Use lubridate robust date handling:
code: # Various date range examples last_week <- ymd(Sys.Date()) - days(7L) today <- ymd(Sys.Date())  # Month of data month_start <- floor_date(Sys.Date(), "month") - months(1L) month_end <- floor_date(Sys.Date(), "month") - days(1L)

title: Getting Started with entsoeapi
text: ENTSO-E endpoints enforce maximum query range one year. package handles transparently automatic pagination:
code: # Query close to the one-year limit year_start <- ymd("2024-01-01", tz = "CET") year_end <- ymd("2024-12-31", tz = "CET")  # The package will handle pagination automatically if needed # This may take longer for large datasets cli_warn("Querying a full year - this may take a moment")  da_prices_year <- energy_prices(   eic = germany_eic,   period_start = year_start,   period_end = year_end,   contract_type = "A01",   tidy_output = TRUE ) #>  #> ── API call ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> → https://web-api.tp.entsoe.eu/api?documentType=A44&in_Domain=10Y1001A1001A82H&out_Domain=10Y1001A1001A82H&periodStart=202312312300&periodEnd=202412302300&contract_MarketAgreement.type=A01&securityToken=<...> #> <- HTTP/2 200  #> <- date: Wed, 08 Apr 2026 13:10:26 GMT #> <- content-type: text/xml #> <- content-disposition: inline; filename="Energy_Prices_202312312300-202412302300.xml" #> <- x-content-type-options: nosniff #> <- x-xss-protection: 0 #> <- vary: accept-encoding #> <- content-encoding: gzip #> <- strict-transport-security: max-age=15724800; includeSubDomains #> <- #> ✔ response has arrived #> ✔ Additional type names have been added! #> ✔ Additional eic names have been added!  cli_alert_success("Retrieved {nrow(da_prices_year)} rows for the year") #> ✔ Retrieved 43800 rows for the year

title: Time Series Restructuring Explained
text: ENTSO-E API encodes time series data compact XML format differs typical “one row per observation” structure used R analysis workflows. vignette explains entsoeapi package transforms compact representation analysis-ready tibbles. vignette covers: compact time series encoding problem tidy_output parameter two formats Curve types A01 (fix sized blocks) A03 (variable sized blocks) Supported time resolutions Practical examples output formats

title: Time Series Restructuring Explained
text: ENTSO-E XML responses encode time series efficiently: Instead storing 24 timestamps, API stores: start time (2024-01-01 00:00:00) resolution (PT60M = 60 minutes = 1 hour) Positions (1, 2, 3, …) mapped actual times Values position compact format efficient data transmission requires reconstruction analysis R.
code: <Period>   <resolution>PT60M</resolution>   <timeInterval>     <start>2024010100000</start>     <end>2024010200000</end>   </timeInterval>   <Point>     <position>1</position>     <price.amount>150.25</price.amount>   </Point>   <Point>     <position>2</position>     <price.amount>152.10</price.amount>   </Point>   <!-- ... 23 more points for hourly data ... --> </Period>

title: Time Series Restructuring Explained
text: functions entsoeapi accept tidy_output parameter:

what: tidy_output = TRUE: One Row Per Point

text: tidy_output = TRUE, row represents single data point: Key columns tidy output:

title: Time Series Restructuring Explained
text: tidy_output = FALSE, row represents time period data points nested list-column: row contains ts_point list-column nested data:

title: Time Series Restructuring Explained
text: Use tidy output need : Plot time series directly ggplot2 Aggregate different time grains (hourly, daily, weekly) Filter specific timestamps Join tidy data Perform standard dplyr operations Use nested output need : Work large datasets efficiently Preserve original API structure Perform operations entire periods Work hierarchical/nested data structures

title: Time Series Restructuring Explained
text: ENTSO-E distinguishes five curve types affect data structured. A01 – SEQUENTIAL FIXED SIZE BLOCKS A02 – POINT A03 – VARIABLE SIZED BLOCK A04 – OVERLAPPING BREAKPOINT A05 – NON-OVERLAPPING BREAKPOINT See details . implemented processing A01 A03 curve types far. 2 proved sufficient till point.

title: Time Series Restructuring Explained
text: Curve type A01 represents regular, evenly-spaced data points: A01 data consistent intervals points (e.g., every 60 minutes).
code: <mRID>1</mRID> <businessType>A01</businessType> <objectAggregation>A08</objectAggregation> <outBiddingZone_Domain.mRID codingScheme="A01">10YFR-RTE------C</outBiddingZone_Domain.mRID> <quantity_Measure_Unit.name>MAW</quantity_Measure_Unit.name> <curveType>A01</curveType> <MktPSRType> <psrType>B10</psrType> </MktPSRType> <Period>   <timeInterval> <start>2020-01-31T23:00Z</start> <end>2020-02-01T23:00Z</end> </timeInterval>   <resolution>PT60M</resolution>   <Point> <position>1</position> <quantity>1466</quantity> </Point>   <Point> <position>2</position> title: Time Series Restructuring Explained
text: Curve type A03 represents variable sized block data positions may absent: A03 data may gaps certain positions package automatically handles A03 data : Building complete positional frame Performing full join identify gaps Carrying forward last observed value (LOCF) fill gaps
code: <mRID>1</mRID> <businessType>A01</businessType> <objectAggregation>A08</objectAggregation> <outBiddingZone_Domain.mRID codingScheme="A01">10YFR-RTE------C</outBiddingZone_Domain.mRID> <quantity_Measure_Unit.name>MAW</quantity_Measure_Unit.name> <curveType>A03</curveType> <MktPSRType> <psrType>B10</psrType> </MktPSRType> <Period>   <timeInterval> <start>2020-01-31T23:00Z</start> <end>2020-02-01T23:00Z</end> </timeInterval>   <resolution>PT60M</resolution>   <Point> <position>1</position> <quantity>1466</quantity> </Point>   <Point> <position>2</position> <quantity>2023</quantity> </Point>   <Point> <position>3</position> <quantity>2365</quantity> </Point>   <Point> <position>4</position> <quantity>3027</quantity> </Point>   <Point> <position>7</position> <quantity>2871</quantity> </Point>   <Point> <position>8</position> <quantity>2179</quantity> </Point>   <Point> <position>9</position> <quantity>1152</quantity> </Point>   <Point> <position>10</position> <quantity>395</quantity> </Point>   <Point> <position>11</position> <quantity>67</quantity> </Point>   <Point> <position>13</position> <quantity>494</quantity> </Point>   <Point> <position>14</position> <quantity>1297</quantity> </Point>   <Point> <position>15</position> <quantity>2230</quantity> </Point>   <Point> <position>16</position> <quantity>2798</quantity> </Point>   <Point> <position>17</position> <quantity>2897</quantity> </Point>   <Point> <position>18</position> <quantity>1572</quantity> </Point>   <Point> <position>19</position> <quantity>1033</quantity> </Point>   <Point> <position>21</position> <quantity>1587</quantity> </Point>   <Point> <position>22</position> <quantity>2058</quantity> </Point>   <Point> <position>23</position> <quantity>1641</quantity> </Point>   <Point> <position>24</position> <quantity>547</quantity> </Point> </Period>

title: Time Series Restructuring Explained
text: ENTSO-E API supports various time resolutions: package automatically calculates timestamps based :
code: timestamp = period_start + (position - 1) × resolution

title: Time Series Restructuring Explained
code: cli_h1("Time Resolution Examples") #>  #> ── Time Resolution Examples ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  # Show data grouped by resolution da_prices_tidy |>   summarize(     points = n(),     start = min(ts_point_dt_start),     end = max(ts_point_dt_start),     .by = ts_resolution   ) |>   kbl(format = "pipe") |>   cat(sep = "\n") #> |ts_resolution | points|start               |end                 | #> |:-------------|------:|:-------------------|:-------------------| #> |PT60M         |     24|2023-12-31 23:00:00 |2024-01-01 22:00:00 |

title: Time Series Restructuring Explained
text: tidy output, aggregation straightforward:
code: cli_h1("Aggregating to Daily Values") #>  #> ── Aggregating to Daily Values ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  da_prices_tidy |>   mutate(date = as.Date(x = ts_point_dt_start, tz = "CET")) |>   summarize(     min_price = min(ts_point_price_amount),     max_price = max(ts_point_price_amount),     mean_price = mean(ts_point_price_amount),     n_points = n(),     .by = date   ) |>   kbl(format = "pipe") |>   cat(sep = "\n") #> |date       | min_price| max_price| mean_price| n_points| #> |:----------|---------:|---------:|----------:|--------:| #> |2024-01-01 |      1.73|     63.33|    36.2425|       24|

title: Time Series Restructuring Explained
text: tidy output, missing points already handled:
code: cli_h1("Checking for Missing Data") #>  #> ── Checking for Missing Data ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  # Check for NA values na_count <- is.na(da_prices_tidy$ts_point_price_amount) |>   sum() cli_text("NA values in price column: {na_count}") #> NA values in price column: 0  # For A03 data, the package fills gaps with LOCF # Check if any positions had gaps filled if ("ts_curve_type" %in% names(da_prices_tidy)) {   a03_count <- sum(da_prices_tidy$ts_curve_type == "A03", na.rm = TRUE)   if (a03_count > 0) {     cli_inform("A03 data: gaps filled using last observation carried forward")   } }

title: Time Series Restructuring Explained
text: tidy_output parameter controls time series data structured: package handles curve types (A01 fix sized blocks, A03 variable sized blocks) common time resolutions automatically.

title: Working with EIC Codes
text: Energy Identification Codes (EICs) 16-character alphanumeric codes uniquely identify market participants, bidding zones, transmission lines, entities ENTSO-E platform. Understanding EICs essential querying data entsoeapi package. vignette covers: EIC code structure validation eight EIC lookup functions Choosing all_approved_eic() all_allocated_eic() Practical examples finding using EIC codes

title: Working with EIC Codes
text: EIC codes follow specific format: Example: 10YDE-VE-------2 10Y - Type code bidding zone (Y = area) DE - Country code (Germany) VE - Area code ------- - Filler characters 2 - Checksum character
code: 10YDE-VE-------2 ^^^^ ^^ ^^^^^^^  ||  ||     ||  ||  ||     ++-- Checksum (weighted-modulo-37)  ||  ++-------- Country/area code  ++------------- Type code

title: Working with EIC Codes
text: first three characters indicate EIC type:

title: Working with EIC Codes
text: package automatically validates EIC codes using ENTSO-E weighted-modulo-37 algorithm. Invalid codes produce error:
code: # EIC with not valid checksum - will fail try(   expr = energy_prices(     eic = "ABCDEF1234567890",     period_start = ymd("2026-01-01", tz = "CET"),     period_end = ymd("2026-01-02", tz = "CET"),     contract_type = "A07",     tidy_output = TRUE   ) ) #> Error in assert_eic(eic = eic) :  #>   Invalid EIC checksum character in `eic` #> ✖ Expected "G", got "0"

what: Combining EIC Data with API Queries
title: Working with EIC Codes
text: Use EIC lookups construct queries:
code: cli_h1("Using EICs in Queries") #>  #> ── Using EICs in Queries ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Query intraday prices for Spain from_ts <- ymd("2026-01-01", tz = "CET") till_ts <- from_ts + days(3L)  es_prices <- energy_prices(   eic = "10YES-REE------0",   period_start = from_ts,   period_end = till_ts,   contract_type = "A07",   tidy_output = TRUE ) #>  #> ── API call ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> → https://web-api.tp.entsoe.eu/api?documentType=A44&in_Domain=10YES-REE------0&out_Domain=10YES-REE------0&periodStart=202512312300&periodEnd=202601032300&contract_MarketAgreement.type=A07&securityToken=<...> #> <- HTTP/2 200  #> <- date: Wed, 08 Apr 2026 13:11:15 GMT #> <- content-type: text/xml #> <- content-disposition: inline; filename="Energy_Prices_202512312300-202601032300.xml" #> <- x-content-type-options: nosniff #> <- x-xss-protection: 0 #> <- vary: accept-encoding #> <- content-encoding: gzip #> <- strict-transport-security: max-age=15724800; includeSubDomains #> <- #> ✔ response has arrived #> ✔ Additional type names have been added! #>  #> ── public download ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ pulling Y_eicCodes.csv file from cache #> ✔ Additional eic names have been added!  cli_alert_success("Retrieved {nrow(es_prices)} intraday price points for Spain") #> ✔ Retrieved 720 intraday price points for Spain  # Show sample data es_prices |>   filter(ts_classification_sequence_position == 1L) |>   select(     market_agreement_type_def,     ts_point_dt_start,     ts_point_price_amount,     ts_currency_unit_name   ) |>   head(12) |>   kbl(format = "pipe") |>   cat(sep = "\n") #> |market_agreement_type_def |ts_point_dt_start   | ts_point_price_amount|ts_currency_unit_name | #> |:-------------------------|:-------------------|---------------------:|:---------------------| #> |Intraday contract         |2025-12-31 23:00:00 |                 93.22|EUR                   | #> |Intraday contract         |2025-12-31 23:15:00 |                 92.83|EUR                   | #> |Intraday contract         |2025-12-31 23:30:00 |                 91.71|EUR                   | #> |Intraday contract         |2025-12-31 23:45:00 |                 91.25|EUR                   | #> |Intraday contract         |2026-01-01 00:00:00 |                 89.37|EUR                   | #> |Intraday contract         |2026-01-01 00:15:00 |                 90.64|EUR                   | #> |Intraday contract         |2026-01-01 00:30:00 |                 88.99|EUR                   | #> |Intraday contract         |2026-01-01 00:45:00 |                 87.08|EUR                   | #> |Intraday contract         |2026-01-01 01:00:00 |                 85.64|EUR                   | #> |Intraday contract         |2026-01-01 01:15:00 |                 86.00|EUR                   | #> |Intraday contract         |2026-01-01 01:30:00 |                 84.97|EUR                   | #> |Intraday contract         |2026-01-01 01:45:00 |                 84.12|EUR                   |

what: Client for the ENTSO-E Transparency Platform API
title: Client for the ENTSO-E Transparency Platform API
text: goal entsoeapi package create easy wrapper around ENTSO-E API’s data transform tabular format without effort. (downloadable data available interactively ENTSO-E transparency platform website well.) package helps displaying queried endpoint URL easier double check upfront checking function arguments’ validity avoid useless API calls query pagination, allowing user worry since package necessary requests unpacking compressed file responses caching data enhance processing speed converting XML structures tabular ones composing consistent detailed outputs providing related, API accessible data (instance: business_types) automatic assigning definitions codes calculating adding timestamps data points (response xml contain information explicitly) implicit_offered_transfer_capacities (11.1) explicit_offered_transfer_capacities (11.1.) continuous_offered_transfer_capacities (11.1) (12.1.E) allocated_transfer_capacities_3rd_countries (12.1.H) load_actual_total (6.1.) load_day_ahead_total_forecast (6.1.B) load_week_ahead_total_forecast (6.1.C) load_month_ahead_total_forecast (6.1.D) load_year_ahead_total_forecast (6.1.E) load_year_ahead_forecast_margin (8.1) gen_installed_capacity_per_pt (14.1.) gen_installed_capacity_per_pu (14.1.B) gen_day_ahead (14.1.C) gen_wind_solar_forecasts (14.1.D) gen_per_gen_unit (16.1.) gen_per_prod_type (16.1.B&C) gen_storage_mean_filling_rate (16.1.D) expansion_and_dismantling_project (9.1) (data available ENTSO-E API yet, 24 March 2026) intraday_cross_border_transfer_limits (11.3) forecasted_transfer_capacities (11.1.) day_ahead_commercial_sched (12.1.F) total_commercial_sched (12.1.F) cross_border_physical_flows (12.1.G) redispatching_internal (13.1.) redispatching_cross_border (13.1.) countertrading (13.1.B) costs_of_congestion_management (13.1.C) outages_cons_units (7.1.&B) outages_fallbacks (IFs 7.2, mFRR 3.11, aFRR 3.10) outages_transmission_grid (10.1.&B) outages_offshore_grid (10.1.C) outages_gen_units (15.1.&B) outages_prod_units (15.1.C&D) outages_both (15.1.&B + 15.1.C&D) exchanged_volumes (aFRR procured_balancing_capacity (12.3.F) allocation_of_cross_zonal_balancing_cap (12.3.H&) (data available ENTSO-E API yet, 24 March 2026) contracted_reserves (17.1.B&C) activated_balancing_prices (17.1.F) imbalance_prices (17.1.G) imbalance_volumes (17.1.H) financial_expenses_and_income (17.1.) fcr_total_capacity (187.2) rr_and_frr_actual_capacity (188.4 & 189.3) shares_of_fcr_capacity (187.2) please submit issue ’ll best resolve . endpoint already implemented, related function gives back empty table, check response XML browser using request URL displayed console just issuing function call. Another verification option might check response Entsoe-e Transparency Platform. case beta test version functions may unique quirks handled yet. please, compare resulting tables data fn Entsoe-e Transparency Platform. title: Client for the ENTSO-E Transparency Platform API
text: can install development version entsoeapi GitHub :
code: if (!require("devtools", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("devtools", quiet = TRUE) devtools::install_github(repo = "krose/entsoeapi", ref = "main")

title: Client for the ENTSO-E Transparency Platform API
text: Read related ENTSO-E Transparency Platform documentation get security token. also create .Renviron file working directory security token call ENTSOE_PAT. ENTSOE_PAT = "your_security_token"
code: if (!require("usethis", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("usethis", quiet = TRUE) usethis::edit_r_environ()

title: Get Accounting Point Z Energy Identification Codes — accounting_point_eic
text: function downloads approved accounting point Z energy identification codes site: https://www.entsoe.eu/data/energy-identification-codes-eic/eic-approved-codes entity balance responsibility balance supplier change can take place commercial business processes defined.

title: Get Accounting Point Z Energy Identification Codes — accounting_point_eic
code: accounting_point_eic()

title: Get Accounting Point Z Energy Identification Codes — accounting_point_eic

title: Display the ENTSO-E Transparency Platform news feed — get_news
code: get_news(feed_url = .feed_url, n = 5L)

title: Display the ENTSO-E Transparency Platform news feed — get_news

title: Display the ENTSO-E Transparency Platform news feed — get_news
text: tibble feed items columns title, pub_date, description, returned invisibly.

title: Display the ENTSO-E Transparency Platform news feed — get_news

This #> newsletter will cover topics such as feature releases, user group meeting announcements, planned events, and any #> service interruptions.To subscribe, please use the following LINK.Kind regards,Transparency Platform team  #>  #> ── PSE: Republication of data under Article 12.1.f  ── #>  #> ℹ Fri, 03 Apr 2026 11:59:36 GMT #> Dear Transparency Platform users,Polskie Sieci Elektroenergetyczne (PSE) hereby informs stakeholders of the #> republication of data released pursuant to Article 12.1.f. The updated dataset now covers the period commencing on 19 #> March 2025. The data have been republished for both directions, and no netting has been applied.We extend our sincere #> apologies for any inconvenience this republication may have caused.Kind regards,Transparency Platform team on behalf of #> PSE #>  #> ── R3.19.0.3 on Thursday 02.04.2026 at 15:00 - 16:30 CEST ── #>  #> ℹ Wed, 01 Apr 2026 14:56:52 GMT #> Dear Transparency Platform Users,The deployment of TP Release R3.19.0.3 on the PROD environment is scheduled to begin #> on Thursday, 2nd April 2026 at 15:00 CEST.Please note that the platform will be unavailable for up to 90 minutes during #> this deployment window.Scope of Release R3.19.0.3:Publications & Filtering:Wrong resolution published in 11.1. Flow #> based processed (PT15M→PT60M)Time Horizon & Source published as codes for 17.1.B&C Volumes and Prices of the Contracted #> Balancing ReservesForecasted Transfer Capacities [11.1] data published with incorrect time zone10.1.A&B Unavailability #> Transmission Grid - Improvements to filters and sortingGUI Exports improvements:10.1.A&B Unavailability in Transmission #> Grid - Unexpected error while downloading data12.3.E - Aggregated Balancing Energy Bids – XML export results in #> errorGUI Performance Improvements:15.1.ABCD - Unavailability of Production & Generation Units chart doesn't #> load"Published Network Elements" data view errorsOther Marker Information[OMI] - unexpected error12.1.D Energy Prices #> for DE‑LU Data view errorREST API improvements:Use of the Transfer Capacity [12.1.A] returns wrong periodsAll Outages 4 #> parameters (periodStart, periodEnd, PeriodStartUpdate, PeriodEndUpdate) are ignored when MRID is included11.1 #> Continuous Evolution - Contract_MarketAgreement.Type should remain an optional parameter13.1.A - Redispatches #> [Internal, Cross Border] , 13.1.B - Contertrading - Corrections to attributes in XML downloads and API responseBest #> regards,Transparency Platform Team #>  #> ── TP PROD Maintenance on 26.03.2026 at 15:00 CET up to 2 hrs ── #>  #> ℹ Wed, 01 Apr 2026 09:58:16 GMT #> Dear Transparency Platform users,Transparency Platform Service Provider will be performing a planned maintenance on #> 26.03.2026 at 15:00 CET with up to 2 hours outage of the TP PROD environment. During outage period, the Transparency #> Platform will not be available.Thank you in advance for your understanding.Best regards,Transparency Platform team #>  #> ── TP PROD Emergency Maintenance on 04.03.2026 at 17:00 CET up to 1hr ── #>  #> ℹ Wed, 01 Apr 2026 09:58:10 GMT #> Dear Transparency Platform users,Due to the performance issue Service Provider will be perform an emergency outage of #> the TP PROD environment.During outage period, the Transparency Platform will not be fully available.The outage will #> take place today at 17:00 CET and is expected to last approximately 60 minutes.Thank you in advance for your #> understanding.Best regards,Transparency Platform team

what: Get Unavailability of Production & Generation Units (15.1.A&B + 15.1.C&D) — outages_both
title: Get Unavailability of Production & Generation Units (15.1.A&B + 15.1.C&D) — outages_both
text: planned forced unavailability production generation units expected last least one market time unit 3 years ahead. "available capacity event" means minimum available generation capacity period specified.

title: Get Unavailability of Production & Generation Units (15.1.A&B + 15.1.C&D) — outages_both
code: outages_both(   eic = NULL,   period_start = ymd(Sys.Date() + days(x = 1L), tz = "CET"),   period_end = ymd(Sys.Date() + days(x = 2L), tz = "CET"),   doc_status = NULL,   event_nature = NULL,   tidy_output = TRUE,   security_token = Sys.getenv("ENTSOE_PAT") )

title: Get Unavailability of Production & Generation Units (15.1.A&B + 15.1.C&D) — outages_both
text: eic Energy Identification Code bidding zone/control area (extract outages bidding zone DE--LU area, recommended send queries per control area .e. CTA|DE(50Hertz), CTA|DE(Amprion), CTA|DE(TeneTGer), CTA|DE(TransnetBW),CTA|,CTA|LU per bidding zone.) period_start starting date -scope period POSIXct YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format One year range limit applies period_end ending date outage -scope period POSIXct YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format One year range limit applies doc_status Notification document status. "A05" active, "A09" cancelled "A13" withdrawn. Defaults NULL means "A05" "A09" together. event_nature "A53" planned maintenance. "A54" unplanned outage. Defaults NULL means . tidy_output Defaults TRUE. flatten nested tables security_token Security token ENTSO-E transparency platform

what: Get Unavailability of Consumption Units. (7.1.A&B) — outages_cons_units
title: Get Unavailability of Consumption Units. (7.1.A&B) — outages_cons_units
text: Unavailability consumption units aggregated form. planned forced outages selected area aggregated according market time unit. list specific consumption units provided.

title: Get Unavailability of Consumption Units. (7.1.A&B) — outages_cons_units
code: outages_cons_units(   eic = NULL,   period_start = ymd(Sys.Date() + days(x = 1L), tz = "CET"),   period_end = ymd(Sys.Date() + days(x = 2L), tz = "CET"),   period_start_update = NULL,   period_end_update = NULL,   doc_status = NULL,   event_nature = NULL,   tidy_output = TRUE,   security_token = Sys.getenv("ENTSOE_PAT") )

title: Get Unavailability of Consumption Units. (7.1.A&B) — outages_cons_units
text: eic Energy Identification Code bidding zone/ control area (extract outages bidding zone DE--LU area, recommended send queries per control area .e. CTA|DE(50Hertz), CTA|DE(Amprion), CTA|DE(TeneTGer), CTA|DE(TransnetBW),CTA|,CTA|LU per bidding zone.) period_start starting date -scope period POSIXct YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format One year range limit applies period_end ending date outage -scope period POSIXct YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format One year range limit applies period_start_update notification submission/update starting date POSIXct YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format period_end_update notification submission/update ending date POSIXct YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format doc_status Notification document status. "A05" active, "A09" cancelled "A13" withdrawn. Defaults NULL means "A05" "A09" together. event_nature "A53" planned maintenance. "A54" unplanned outage. Defaults NULL means . tidy_output Defaults TRUE. flatten nested tables security_token Security token ENTSO-E transparency platform

title: Get Fall-Back Procedures. (IFs IN 7.2, mFRR 3.11, aFRR 3.10) — outages_fallbacks
text: publishes application fall back procedures participants European platforms result disconnection DSO European platform, unavailability European platform (planned unplanned outage) situation algorithm used platform fails find solution.

title: Get Fall-Back Procedures. (IFs IN 7.2, mFRR 3.11, aFRR 3.10) — outages_fallbacks
code: outages_fallbacks(   eic = NULL,   period_start = ymd(Sys.Date() - days(x = 7L), tz = "CET"),   period_end = ymd(Sys.Date(), tz = "CET"),   process_type = "A63",   event_nature = "A53",   tidy_output = TRUE,   security_token = Sys.getenv("ENTSOE_PAT") )

what: Get Unavailability of Generation Units. (15.1.A&B) — outages_gen_units
title: Get Unavailability of Generation Units. (15.1.A&B) — outages_gen_units
text: planned forced unavailability generation units expected last least one market time unit 3 years ahead. "available capacity event" means minimum available generation capacity period specified.

title: Get Unavailability of Generation Units. (15.1.A&B) — outages_gen_units
code: outages_gen_units(   eic = NULL,   period_start = ymd(Sys.Date() + days(x = 1L), tz = "CET"),   period_end = ymd(Sys.Date() + days(x = 2L), tz = "CET"),   doc_status = NULL,   event_nature = NULL,   tidy_output = TRUE,   security_token = Sys.getenv("ENTSOE_PAT") )

title: Get Unavailability of Generation Units. (15.1.A&B) — outages_gen_units
text: eic Energy Identification Code bidding zone/ control area (extract outages bidding zone DE--LU area, recommended send queries per control area .e. CTA|DE(50Hertz), CTA|DE(Amprion), CTA|DE(TeneTGer),CTA|DE(TransnetBW), CTA|,CTA|LU per bidding zone.) period_start starting date -scope period POSIXct YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format One year range limit applies period_end ending date outage -scope period POSIXct YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format One year range limit applies doc_status Notification document status. "A05" active, "A09" cancelled "A13" withdrawn. Defaults NULL means "A05" "A09" together. event_nature "A53" planned maintenance. "A54" unplanned outage. Defaults NULL means . tidy_output Defaults TRUE. flatten nested tables security_token Security token ENTSO-E transparency platform

title: Get Unavailability of Generation Units. (15.1.A&B) — outages_gen_units

title: Get Unavailability of Offshore Grid Infrastructure. (10.1.C) — outages_offshore_grid
text: Unavailability -shore grid reduce wind power feed-least one market time unit. Wind power fed time change availability provided.

title: Get Unavailability of Offshore Grid Infrastructure. (10.1.C) — outages_offshore_grid
code: outages_offshore_grid(   eic = NULL,   period_start = ymd(Sys.Date() + days(x = 1L), tz = "CET"),   period_end = ymd(Sys.Date() + days(x = 2L), tz = "CET"),   doc_status = NULL,   tidy_output = TRUE,   security_token = Sys.getenv("ENTSOE_PAT") )

title: Get Unavailability of Offshore Grid Infrastructure. (10.1.C) — outages_offshore_grid
text: eic Energy Identification Code bidding zone/ control area (extract outages bidding zone DE--LU area, recommended send queries per control area .e. CTA|DE(50Hertz), CTA|DE(Amprion), CTA|DE(TeneTGer), CTA|DE(TransnetBW),CTA|,CTA|LU per bidding zone.) period_start starting date -scope period POSIXct YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format One year range limit applies period_end ending date outage -scope period POSIXct YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format One year range limit applies doc_status Notification document status. NULL "A05" active "A13" withdrawn. Defaults NULL. tidy_output Defaults TRUE. flatten nested tables security_token Security token ENTSO-E transparency platform

what: Get Unavailability of Production Units. (15.1.C&D) — outages_prod_units
title: Get Unavailability of Production Units. (15.1.C&D) — outages_prod_units
text: planned forced unavailability production units expected last least one market time unit 3 years ahead. "available capacity event" means minimum available generation capacity period specified.

title: Get Unavailability of Production Units. (15.1.C&D) — outages_prod_units
code: outages_prod_units(   eic = NULL,   period_start = ymd(Sys.Date() + days(x = 1L), tz = "CET"),   period_end = ymd(Sys.Date() + days(x = 2L), tz = "CET"),   doc_status = NULL,   event_nature = NULL,   tidy_output = TRUE,   security_token = Sys.getenv("ENTSOE_PAT") )

title: Get Unavailability of Production Units. (15.1.C&D) — outages_prod_units
text: eic Energy Identification Code bidding zone/ control area (extract outages bidding zone DE--LU area, recommended send queries per control area .e. CTA|DE(50Hertz), CTA|DE(Amprion), CTA|DE(TeneTGer), CTA|DE(TransnetBW),CTA|,CTA|LU per bidding zone.) period_start starting date -scope period POSIXct YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format One year range limit applies period_end ending date outage -scope period POSIXct YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format One year range limit applies doc_status Notification document status. "A05" active, "A09" cancelled "A13" withdrawn. Defaults NULL means "A05" "A09" together. event_nature "A53" planned maintenance. "A54" unplanned outage. Defaults NULL means . tidy_output Defaults TRUE. flatten nested tables security_token Security token ENTSO-E transparency platform

what: Check if the ENTSO-E API provider is reachable — there_is_provider
title: Check if the ENTSO-E API provider is reachable — there_is_provider
text: Sends probe request ENTSO-E Transparency Platform API returns TRUE server responds HTTP 401 Unauthorized (meaning endpoint dummy token rejected). Returns FALSE internet connection server unreachable. Primarily intended @examplesIf guard package documentation.

title: entsoeapi v1.1.1 (2026-04-08)
text: None.

title: entsoeapi v1.1.1 (2026-04-08)
text: queries allowed capture 1 year data maximum time span. leap year handling elaborated yet. version leap year cases going handled nicely. Typos fixed documentations. ENTSOE_PAT going checked UUID v4 format now . vignettes slightly adjusted.

title: entsoeapi v1.1.1 (2026-04-08)
text: code base unit tests adjusted way makes whole project maintainable.

title: entsoeapi v1.1.0 (2026-03-31)
text: 4 new vignettes (Generation Mix Analysis, Getting Started entsoeapi, Time Series Restructuring Explained, Working EIC Codes) introduced. all_allocated_eic() function exported. get_news() function introduced. ENTSO-E API known returning 503 (Service Unavailable) response ’s heavy load client sends many requests. Hence built-automated backoff--retry mechanism.

title: entsoeapi v1.1.0 (2026-03-31)
text: Typos fixed documentations. package DESCRIPTION document slightly adjusted.

title: entsoeapi v1.1.0 (2026-03-31)
text: orphaned, exported function related unit tests removed.

title: entsoeapi v1.0.0 (2026-03-25)
text: implicit_offered_transfer_capacities() function introduced (incorrectly stated earlier function introduced) explicit_offered_transfer_capacities() function introduced (incorrectly stated earlier function introduced) continuous_offered_transfer_capacities() function introduced (incorrectly stated earlier function introduced) netted_volumes_per_border() function introduced (incorrectly stated earlier function introduced) eic_functions() all_allocated_eic() functions introduced. processing capability P3M data resolution added engine.

title: entsoeapi v1.0.0 (2026-03-25)
text: aggregated_balancing_energy_bids() function’s process_type argument additionally can set A67 A68 now . “See Also” blocks added help. result table imbalance_prices() call got columns display. deactivation_requested_date_and_or_time_date eic_code_market_participant_street_address columns added all_allocated_eic() function’s resulting table. gen_installed_capacity_per_pt gen_installed_capacity_per_pu functions got new tidy_output argument. XML extracting engine improved flow_based_allocations function’s resulting table composed properly now . tibble, purrr, tidyselect package dependencies (Imports) removed code.

title: entsoeapi v1.0.0 (2026-03-25)
text: day_ahead_prices() function renamed energy_prices() allows query day-ahead intraday prices . resulting table’s column names converted snake case party_eic(), area_eic(), accounting_point_eic(), tie_line_eic(), location_eic(), resource_object_eic(), substation_eic() all_approved_eic() function. financial_expenses_and_income_for_balancing() renamed financial_expenses_and_income(). sharing_of_frr_capacity() renamed sharing_of_rr_and_frr_capacity(). rr_actual_capacity() removed since rr_and_frr_actual_capacity() covers functionality. business_type argument removed shares_of_fcr_capacity() since changed result table . business_type argument removed rr_and_frr_actual_capacity() since changed result table . gen_wind_solar_forecasts() give back list table process_type now ; instead process_type moved arguments function call.
